The simple, spiritual living in Andhra’s Kurma Gramam village
description: Nestled amidst the scenic hills of Hiramandalam in Andhra Pradesh, Kurma Gramam is a tiny hamlet, spread across 60 acres and inhabited by 17 families that stands in contrast to modern lifestyles. Cut off from the contemporary living, the village functions without electricity, mobile phones, internet connectivity or even LPG services. Life here moves at a slower pace, deeply rooted in simplicity and spiritual discipline, writes Harish Gilai

title: A tribute to India: Inside chef Kunal Kapur’s new restaurant in Bengaluru
description: With Pincode, the celebrity chef and Masterchef  host, is bringing dishes from different parts of the country to the table, from mutton ribs inji puli to Himalayan thukpa. Over a leisurely lunch, he takes us through the menu

description: "We did not seek war and we do not want it," Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in the message read out on state TV

In Punjab, parents to get daily SMS alerts about kids’ school attendance, absence
Summer scares: Human-animal conflict in Kollam’s forest fringes
description: As summer progresses, the eastern forest fringes of Kollam become hotspots of human-wildlife conflict. The presence of herds of bison and elephants even near towns throws normal life into disarray. The crisis extends into the vast agricultural sectors of the region as well. Navamy Sudhish explores the reasons for the wildlife incursion and how effective are the preventive strategies deployed by the Forest department

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
description: Stock market recommendations: IndusInd Bank, and IIFL Finance - these are the two stocks that Bajaj Broking Research has recommended for buying today with a one month time horizon. The brokerage has also shared its views and outlook for Nifty, and Bank Nifty.

title: Electricity bills of up to Rs 9 lakh: Phase 11 residents express outrage over inflated power charges
As Punjab sees 471% surplus rain in a week, concerns crop around wheat harvest
Netanyahu reacts after Pakistan Defence Minister calls ‘to get rid of European Jews’ amid peace talks
Mukul Choudhary, Avesh Khan script history with record stand in LSG’s last-ball heist
description: Mukul Choudhary and Avesh Khan's record-breaking 54-run partnership for the eighth wicket secured a thrilling three-wicket victory for Lucknow Super Giants against Kolkata Knight Riders.  Chasing 182, LSG were in trouble at 128/7 before Choudhary's explosive hitting, including seven sixes, and Khan's support sealed the win on the final ball, marking KKR's struggles at the death.

title: Punjab Kings opener | With strike rate being 137.93, Prabhsimran dreams big: ‘Scoring 250 runs is not that difficult anymore’
Bid to clean up a rotten trail in Hyderabad’s thriving food scene
description: Beneath Hyderabad’s booming food economy lies a dark web of adulteration, mislabelling and unsafe practices. Of late, an unprecedented police-led crackdown is pulling back the curtain on a sprawling food fraud network that thrives on gaps in regulation and consumer trust. But as enforcement tightens and complaints surge, the city finds itself trying not just to fix a broken food chain, but also to restore faith in what lands on every plate, write Serish Nanisetti and Lavpreet Kaur

title: 'Will advance management of Hormuz to new stage': Khamenei flags shift amid fragile truce
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has vowed to escalate control over the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a tougher stance amid a strained ceasefire with the US and Israel. He declared Iran would seek compensation for wartime losses and warned aggressors would not go unpunished. Khamenei also claimed victory in the conflict, urging continued public pressure during ongoing negotiations.

title: ‘Destiny is still in our hands’: Rovman Powell remains upbeat after KKR’s defeat to LSG in IPL 2026
Two India-bound ships among a few transiting Hormuz Strait on Day 2 of ceasefire
description: Ship tracker websites show that Marivex, an oil tanker, is carrying oil from Bandar Abbas in Iran to New Mangalore Port; MSG, another oil tanker, is carrying cargo from UAE to India

title: In breakthrough, cheap aluminium may replace costly catalysts in pharma
description: New research has found aluminium can be made to behave in the same way that makes transition metals such good catalysts; the stakes are high for India, which has abundant aluminium but currently meets its transition metal needs almost entirely through imports

title: ‘I ignored period pain, then my organs got stuck together’: How a 31-year-old teacher beat endometriosis to birth a child
Bengaluru: Wide roads, narrow chance?
description: After years of failing to widen roads, the five city corporations have announced a renewed push for these projects this year. But with land losers still resisting compensation through TDR, and the corporations in no position to pay cash, will the projects take off or will the impasse continue, checks Chetan B.C.

title: 30% of voters in a Murshidabad seat removed during adjudication: What EC data on Bengal deletions reveal

OpenAI puts the UK Stargate project on hold, and says will return when conditions are right
description: OpenAI has halted its ambitious Stargate AI project in the United Kingdom. High energy prices and regulatory uncertainties are cited as reasons for the pause. The company sees potential in the UK's AI future but needs favorable conditions for investment. This decision impacts Prime Minister Keir Starmer's plans to boost AI development in Britain.

title: How women’s self-help group’s legal battle led to scrapping of Rs 60-lakh tender in Chhattisgarh
Bengal voter lists frozen, only 2 of 27.16 lakh deleted known to have got tribunal hearing
Jeff Pearlman calls out media over Dianna Russini controversy
description: Photos of Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel at a resort sparked widespread speculation despite no confirmed wrongdoing. Jeff Pearlman highlighted a clear double standard, arguing a male reporter would not face similar scrutiny. While defending Russini’s credibility, he also noted the professional risks tied to perception. The episode reflects deeper challenges women in sports media continue to face, where optics often carry more weight than facts.

title: BSF opens up Assam gates for families living beyond Bangladesh border fence to vote
description: Border Security Force personnel facilitated voting for 70 families in Assam's Sribhumi district, living beyond the India-Bangladesh barbed-wire fence.  Vehicles and opened gates ensured residents of five villages could cast their ballots, a powerful assertion of their Indian identity.  BSF also aided election campaigning in these remote settlements near the Zero Line.

title: ‘Rishabh bhaiya told me not to think too much’: Mukul Choudhary opens up about Rishabh Pant’s advice after his unbeaten 54 off 27 balls against KKR in IPL 2026

‘Breach of most intimate trust’: Gauhati High Court spares life of man who killed his family
Candidates Chess: India’s R Vaishali takes sole lead with four rounds to go
description: India's Vaishali Rameshbabu leads the FIDE Candidates 2026 women's section after a draw in the 10th round, showcasing impressive form. However, compatriots Divya Deshmukh and R Praggnanandhaa faced setbacks. Divya's endgame collapse likely ended her championship hopes, while Praggnanandhaa's tactical misstep against Sindarov cost him dearly, impacting India's overall challenge.

title: Setback for arms consultant, Delhi HC upholds 'fugitive offender' tag
description: Delhi High Court has affirmed a trial court's decision labeling UK-based arms consultant Sanjay Bhandari a "fugitive economic offender" in a money laundering case. The court dismissed Bhandari's appeal, stating that a failed extradition attempt does not absolve him of Indian law violations. This ruling allows authorities to confiscate his assets.

The thrilling chase culminated in an emotional embrace between Choudhary and LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka, a moment that resonated widely and highlighted the team's burgeoning talent and spirit.

This aligns with sentiments from tech leaders like OpenAI's Sam Altman and JPMorgan Chase's Jamie Dimon, who also foresee significant reductions in working hours due to AI advancements.

title: Lions linked to $94m trade to boost Aidan Hutchinson’s rise
description: The Detroit Lions are exploring ways to strengthen their defensive line around Aidan Hutchinson, with Jeffery Simmons emerging as a potential trade target. Simmons’ elite interior presence could transform Detroit’s pass rush, but the cost of draft capital raises valid concerns. With a Super Bowl window open, the Lions must weigh immediate impact against long-term flexibility in deciding whether to pursue a blockbuster move.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ earns Rs 1,665 cr in 3 weeks, misses ‘Dhurandhar’ record
description: 'Dhurandhar 2' experiences a slowdown, concluding its third week with Rs 110.60 crore. Despite surpassing its predecessor's lifetime earnings, the spy action film failed to break 'Dhurandhar's' third-week collection record. The movie's domestic net collections reached Rs 1,048.42 crore, with a global gross of Rs 1,665.23 crore.

title: MP tribal women on 'funeral pyres' oppose river linking

She delivers short speeches, indirectly criticizing AIADMK leaders and seeking public support, while her party fields candidates, primarily in southern districts, aiming for crucial votes and recalling her close association with Jayalalithaa.

RG Kar victim's mom enters fray amid 'we want justice' slogans
description: The mother of the RG Kar rape-murder victim, a BJP candidate, filed her nomination wearing a saree inscribed with 'Shaktirupena Sangsthita'. Accompanied by Smriti Irani and Biplab Deb, she reiterated her call to oust the TMC government, seeking justice for women in Bengal. Her movable assets are valued at Rs 19 lakh.

title: Kashmir scientists crack cultivation of rare Rs 40,000 morel mushrooms
PM Modi puts fish on poll menu to counter Mamata's attack
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress of failing to boost fish production in Bengal, a state known for its love of 'maachh-bhaat'. He highlighted BJP's focus on fisheries development and contrasted TMC's alleged governance of fear with BJP's trust-building. Mamata Banerjee retorted, asserting Bengal's self-sufficiency in fish and questioning BJP's stance on dietary choices.

title: Karnataka Information Commission riled over government not recovering penalty from salary of officials denying info under RTI Act
description: So far, only ₹2.70 crore has been recovered from 3,084 Public Information Officers while the KIC has imposed a fine of ₹10.38 crores on 10,843 government officials designated as PIOs

title: India finalising agreement to supply oil, gas to Mauritius amid West Asia crisis: Jaishankar
description: The West Asia war and Iran’s chokehold of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which a fifth of global oil passes, had halted shipping and sent global oil prices soaring

title: 27 lakh out of Bengal rolls after adjudication, maximum from Muslim, Matua belts
description: Murshidabad's Shamsherganj and Lalgola constituencies saw the highest voter deletions post-adjudication, with 74,775 and 55,420 names removed respectively. This comes amidst a larger purge of 27 lakh voters across Bengal, sparking concerns and protests, particularly in Muslim-dominant and Matua-dominated areas. Electoral rolls for the second phase have been frozen, with a court review pending.

title: ‘Your voice matters’: Delhi LG urges public to send grievances via now-revived ‘LG Listening Post’ platform
97-year-old 1947 refugee, presiding officer in 12 polls, struck off voter rolls in Bengal
description: Bengal's electoral rolls are in disarray as long-time voters, including a 97-year-old Partition refugee and a retired teacher, find their names struck off. Families are battling bureaucratic hurdles and stress, with one man dying in a tribunal queue. The process has left many disenfranchised, sparking protests and accusations of political manipulation.

title: Cowboys eye $6m WR trade; chiefs, raiders, chargers in mix
description: The Dallas Cowboys face a tough call on George Pickens after his breakout season. Despite elite production, contract concerns and off-field questions cloud his future. With extension talks uncertain, teams like the Kansas City Chiefs, Las Vegas Raiders, and Los Angeles Chargers could pursue a trade. Dallas may ultimately move its rising star rather than commit long-term money.

title: Public morality must govern matters of faith: Centre to Supreme Court on Sabarimala

After Venu Srinivasan, Tata Trusts trustee Vijay Singh calls for listing of Tata Sons

'Journey started even before I was born': Mukul after winning game for LSG
description: Mukul Choudhary emerged as LSG’s hero with a match-winning unbeaten 54 off 27 balls in a last-ball win over KKR. Reflecting on his journey, he said, "Our financial condition wasn’t strong," delaying his start in cricket. Staying calm under pressure, he added, "My plan was simple - I wanted to stay till the end." Known for his attacking style, he credited belief and patience, saying he always backed himself to finish the game.

title: Assam, Puducherry record highest-ever voter turnouts
description: Record voter turnouts were recorded in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, with officials hailing it as a validation of electoral roll revisions and a sign of public trust. Assam and Puducherry achieved all-time highs, while Kerala saw its best turnout since 1987. Muslim-majority seats in Assam saw a significant surge in participation.

description: The Afghan spinner was referring to nine scalps in 15 matches at an economy of 9.34. It may have made some wonder whether his magic was on the wane

title: CWC meet over women's bill today
description: Congress Working Committee will convene Friday to deliberate on the women's reservation law amendments, following DMK chief M.K. Stalin's sharp critique. Concerns are raised that a "proportionate increase" in Lok Sabha seats could disadvantage southern and smaller states, benefiting northern ones. The timing of the BJP's proposal amid assembly elections also faces scrutiny.

title: Fragile truce in place, India reaches out to Gulf nations
description: India is bolstering its engagement with the Gulf as a fragile ceasefire takes hold in West Asia. Union Minister S. Jaishankar is visiting the UAE to deepen strategic ties, while Minister Hardeep Singh Puri is in Qatar to secure vital energy supplies.

title: Padikkal glad the work on improving strike-rate is paying off
description: The RCB batter stressed batting was not just about the strike rate

title: With just an hour to go, Congress pulls out of Baramati race
description: In a significant political maneuver, the Congress party withdrew its candidate from the Baramati bye-election just before the nomination deadline. This decision, influenced by appeals from the Pawar family and Mahayuti partners, ensures Sunetra Pawar faces no major opposition. The bypoll was necessitated by the tragic passing of Ajit Pawar.

'They better stop now': Trump warns Iran over Strait of Hormuz tanker fees
description: President Donald Trump issued stern warnings to Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz, accusing Tehran of hindering oil shipments. He also criticized The Wall Street Journal's reporting on his Iran policy, asserting his actions have prevented Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons and will soon restore oil flow. These statements follow a recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran.

description: M.L. Ninama, who recently took voluntary retirement three months before his scheduled superannuation, joined the BJP at an event in Shamlaji in Aravalli district

description: The BJP initially capitalised on Hindutva politics in Kanniyakumari, winning its first Assembly seat in 1996; thereafter it tasted success only in alliance with the Dravidian majors; hopes to convert its rising vote share to more seats in 2026

title: Govt plans design, development of unmanned combat search and rescue aircraft for IAF
description: The Indian Air Force is set to receive an indigenous unmanned combat search and rescue aircraft. This autonomous system will be capable of carrying 400 kg, including passengers and stretchers, and can deliver supplies to challenging terrains.

title: Army contingent departs for Egypt for joint special forces exercise
description: An Indian Army special forces contingent has arrived in Egypt for the fourth edition of the joint exercise 'Cyclone-IV'. This collaboration, running from April 9-17, aims to bolster military ties and enhance joint mission planning.  Twenty-five Indian soldiers will train alongside Egyptian counterparts, focusing on special operational tactics in desert environments to improve interoperability and share best practices.

title: Gleaming interiors, building with green features: Showcase Noida airport prepares to welcome flights
Putin announces truce with Ukraine during Orthodox Easter celebrations
description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has announced a temporary ceasefire with Ukraine for the Orthodox Easter holidays, effective from April 11th to April 12th, 2026. This move follows a similar holiday truce proposal from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky. The Kremlin stated that Russian troops are instructed to cease combat operations but remain ready to counter any enemy provocations.

description: A new international study reveals lunar water ice deposits in permanently shadowed regions are more stable than previously thought, with 74% of these areas unaffected by impacts. This finding is crucial for future manned lunar missions aiming to establish bases, as these regions offer promising targets for water ice exploration and utilization.

This ruling allows the Department of Defense to continue excluding Anthropic's technology from military contracts, citing national security concerns. While Anthropic faces financial harm, the court prioritized government access to vital AI during conflict.

description: Bharat Electronics Limited is establishing a new manufacturing unit in Uttar Pradesh's defence corridor, investing over Rs 600 crore. This facility will produce next-generation missiles, radar systems, and India's own 'Iron Dome,' aiming to boost indigenous defence capabilities and reduce foreign dependency. The project is expected to create significant employment and foster industrial growth in the state.

title: 'Sold to street vendors at Rs 20/litre': UP factory sealed for making adulterated tomato sauce
description: A food manufacturing unit in Hapur was sealed after a raid uncovered the production of tomato sauce under unhygienic conditions using vegetable pulp, acid, and banned synthetic colours. Officials seized nearly 200 litres of adulterated sauce, allegedly sold cheaply to local eateries and street vendors.

IPL: Mukul snatches win from KKR’s clutches in last-ball thriller
description: Lucknow Super Giants beat Kolkata Knight Riders by three wickets in a last-ball thriller, chasing 182. KKR scored 181/4 with key knocks from Ajinkya Rahane and Angkrish Raghuvanshi. In reply, Ayush Badoni’s 54 and Mukul Choudhary’s unbeaten 54 sealed the win. Despite wickets from Anukul Roy and Vaibhav Arora, KKR couldn’t stop LSG in a tense finish.

title: Iran-US truce talks in Pakistan: Who is likely to attend?
description: A high-level US delegation, led by Vice President JD Vance, will travel to Islamabad this weekend for crucial talks with Iran. The discussions aim to secure a more lasting ceasefire and end weeks of intense hostilities. Pakistan will host the in-person meetings, with Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif expected to be involved. Both sides are set to hold direct negotiations.
